channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Foods and Habits Wey Fit Add to Your Cancer Risk, According to Research
item.description: This article by Gloria Adesanya for Pulse Nigeria explore how some everyday foods fit raise a person’s chance of developing cancer. Scientists don show say around 20% of cancers dey linked to lifestyl...

item.title: UK Condemns Iran After Missiles Fired at Diego Garcia Base
item.description: The United Kingdom has strongly condemned Iran for its recent missile attacks on the joint US-UK military base at Diego Garcia, located in the Chagos Islands. The attacks, although unsuccessful, have ...

item.title: 93-Year-Old Music Teacher Continues Inspiring Students with Passion and Curiosity
item.description: At 93, Mary Ann Cummins is still an active music teacher, teaching at multiple schools and as a private piano instructor in Santa Monica. She began her teaching career in 1971, when she was recruited ...

item.title: Gas Explosions in Lagos and Calabar Kill Two, Damage Millions in Property
item.description: Two gas-related explosions rocked southern Nigeria over the weekend, claiming at least two lives and leaving multiple people injured. The first incident occurred in Calabar at Fomex filling station al...

item.title: Iraq Restarts Iranian Gas Imports at Reduced Rate After Three-Day Halt
item.description: Iraq's Electricity Ministry announced on Saturday the gradual resumption of gas supplies from Iran following a three-day interruption. The restart came after Iran halted shipments in response to an Is...

item.title: TOWIE star Jordan Brook diagnosed with viral meningitis and encephalitis, shares recovery update from hospital
item.description: Reality television personality Jordan Brook, known from The Only Way Is Essex (TOWIE), has been hospitalised with viral meningitis and encephalitis — two serious conditions involving inflammation of t...

item.title: Filipino Navy worker details recruitment into China-linked espionage scheme
item.description: The article features an interview with a Filipino defense worker, given the pseudonym Lawrence, who admits to having spied for Chinese handlers while employed in the Philippine national security secto...

item.title: Exploring potential solutions to global challenges
item.description: The article poses a thought-provoking question about finding the best solution to the world’s complex problems, which range from climate change and geopolitical tensions to inequality and global healt...

item.title: Jury Finds Musk Liable for Misleading Twitter Investors in 2022 Buyout
item.description: A San Francisco federal court jury ruled on March 20, 2026, that Elon Musk intentionally misled Twitter shareholders prior to his $44-billion acquisition of the platform in 2022, now known as X. The e...

item.title: Zanele Muholi Awarded Prestigious Hasselblad Prize for Visual Activism
item.description: South African visual activist Zanele Muholi, who uses they/them pronouns, has received the world's largest photography prize, the Hasselblad Award, from the Hasselblad Foundation in Sweden. This honou...

item.title: Eugene Wamalwa Addresses the Controversy Around His Brother's Estate
item.description: A video of Eugene Wamalwa, leader of the Democratic Action Party-Kenya (DAP-K), has emerged in which he addresses the ongoing legal dispute surrounding his late brother Kijana Wamalwa’s estate. The is...

item.title: Asake Talks About His Journey and Loyalty to Olamide
item.description: Asake don open up for Korty EO podcast about how he suffer before he blow under YBNL and how Olamide change him life. He talk say 2020 be one tough year for am, the time when he release ‘Mr Money’ but...

item.title: Rajnath Singh Says India Has Effectively Managed Fuel and Fertiliser Challenges Amid West Asia Conflict
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh stated in Haldwani on Saturday that India has been successfully managing the potential impacts of the ongoing conflict in West Asia, including possible energy and fertil...

item.title: Over 12,000 Federal Jobs to be Eliminated in Cost-Cutting Review
item.description: The Canadian federal government is set to cut over 12,000 full-time equivalent (FTE) positions over the next three years, as part of a larger cost-cutting review aimed at saving billions of dollars. T...

item.title: Escalating Conflict: Iran Strikes US-UK Base as Tensions with Israel Grow
item.description: The conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance has intensified, with Iran launching ballistic missiles at the Diego Garcia military base, a joint US-UK facility in the Indian Ocean. The strikes ...

item.title: Iran targets Diego Garcia military base in failed missile attack
item.description: Iran has attempted an attack on the joint US-UK military base on Diego Garcia, located in the Chagos Islands. Two ballistic missiles were reportedly fired at the base, but neither reached its target. ...

item.title: Brighton Defeat Liverpool as Hugo Ekitike Leaves Early Through Injury
item.description: Liverpool suffered another setback in their pursuit of a top-five finish in the Premier League as they lost 2-1 to Brighton at the Amex Stadium. The match took a turn for the worse within the first ei...

item.title: Oryx Gas Tanzania Supports 120 Children with Eid ul Fitr Donations
item.description: In a heartwarming initiative, Oryx Gas Tanzania has donated food items to the New Faraja Orphanage Centre in Mburahati, Dar es Salaam, benefiting 120 children. This donation, intended to assist with p...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Winning Numbers for March 21, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) has released the official lotto results for Saturday, March 21, 2026. As part of its commitment to transparency and public service, ABS-CBN News publis...

item.title: Controversy over Large-Scale Solar Farms in Lincolnshire
item.description: Lincolnshire is at the centre of a heated dispute over large-scale solar farms, with more approved projects than anywhere else in the UK. Energy Secretary Ed Miliband supports the expansion of solar p...
